title: Double-cooked Brazilian fish Nigiri
description: A sweet and crisp take on traditional sushi, featuring a Brazilian twist and a fun nigiri-shaping process.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: For adding a sweet touch to the cod fillets

ingredients.name: Rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: For adding a tangy flavor to the rice

instructions: Cut the cod fillets into small pieces and set aside.
In a shallow dish, mix together the tapioca starch, salt, and black pepper.
Coat each piece of cod in the tapioca starch mixture, shaking off any excess.
Heat the palm oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the coated cod pieces to the skillet and fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Remove the cod from the skillet and set aside to cool.
Prepare the sushi rice according to the package instructions.
Once the rice is cooked, mix in the sugar and rice vinegar, and set aside to cool.
Cut the cooled rice into small pieces, and shape into nigiri using a small amount of water to help hold the shape.
Place a small piece of fried cod on top of each nigiri, and garnish with sesame seeds and cilantro leaves.
